title: Sequences, Recurrences, and Discrete Change · Decision Defense
summary: Forecast savings, inventory, and staged population processes. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a discrete recurrence forecast. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent explicit and recursive sequences and state valid index domains.
Model constant differences and constant ratios with arithmetic and geometric sequences.
Calculate finite sums and explain their accumulated quantity and units.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 19 units, changes by −6 units, then by +2 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Discrete Stability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect long-run behavior and sensitivity of discrete models.
